Get started with my free cleaning guide ⬇️

externalUrls.title: Tap here for all the links✨

latestPosts.caption:
✨️If you’re new here, this is the best place to start.⁠
⁠
Most people think they need more time, more motivation, or a full day to get their home under control.⁠
You don’t. You need a simple rhythm you can follow without overthinking it.⁠
⁠
That’s exactly what the Clean Mama System is designed to give you.⁠
Save and scroll to find out:⁠
⁠
✨️ where to start⁠
✨️ how the routine works⁠
✨️ and how to build a home that stays manageable⁠
⁠
Start with the free guide, then take the next step when you’re ready.⁠
Comment GUIDE and I’ll send you the brand-new free guide.⁠
Save this so you can come back to it anytime.⁠
⁠
ps - Homekeeping Society opens soon — this is the best place to begin.

🚽If your bathroom never feels fully clean, it’s not because you’re not trying…

➡️It’s because the order matters more than the effort.

Get my guide that walks you through exactly what to clean and when so you’re not undoing your own work.

Comment BATHROOM and I’ll send it to you.

If your house feels out of control, start here.⁠
Not with deep cleaning.⁠
Not with organizing.⁠
Not with catching up.⁠
Start with a simple rhythm.⁠
⁠
The Clean Mama Routine shows you exactly:⁠
⁠
✔️what to do daily⁠
✔️what to do weekly⁠
✔️how to stay consistent⁠
✔️how to stop guessing⁠
⁠
This is the system that keeps homes manageable without spending hours ⁠
cleaning.⁠
Comment GUIDE and I’ll send it to you or grab it at cleanmama.com/subscribe.⁠
Small tasks + a clear plan = less deciding⁠

I don’t wash my sheets on Saturdays because Saturdays are magical.

I do it because I don’t want to think about it.
Every week, the same task happens on the same day. No reminders. No wondering how long it’s been. No decision fatigue.

That’s the real magic of the Clean Mama Routine. It removes hundreds of tiny decisions so your home keeps moving without relying on motivation.
Today’s task? Fresh sheets and towels.

✔️Comment GUIDE and I’ll send you my free guide! 

🗓️Save this as your reminder—or better yet, make Saturday your sheet day too.

What if the secret isn't cleaning more...but deciding less?⁠
⁠
The biggest changes in my home didn't come from buying more organizers or spending entire weekends cleaning. They came from creating simple systems for the things I do every single day.⁠
⁠
✨ A place for the mail.⁠
✨ A routine for laundry.⁠
✨ A landing spot for keys and shoes.⁠
✨ A Sunday reset.⁠
⁠
Each one removes a few more decisions from my day, and together they make my home feel calmer, easier to manage, and so much less overwhelming.⁠
⁠
Isn’t that the real goal?⁠
Not a perfect home.⁠
A home that works for you.⁠
⁠
✨ Comment HOURS and I'll send you my blog post with all 10 Home Systems, plus my free Homekeeping Guide to help you get started.⁠
⁠
✨Save this post so you can come back to it as you build one simple system at a time.

Paper clutter isn’t usually about too much mail. It’s about too many decisions.

Every envelope asks a question:
• Do I need this? • Should I pay it? • Should I keep it? • Where does it go?

When there isn’t a simple answer, paper ends up on the counter “for later.”

I don’t think of this as organizing—it’s a system.

Every piece of mail gets one decision: 📬 Recycle 📂 Action 🗃️ File

Once those decisions are made, the clutter never has a chance to build.

Small systems like this are what make a home feel calmer. They don’t eliminate tasks—they eliminate the constant mental load of wondering what to do next.

What’s one area of your home that needs a better system instead of more organizing?

✔️Comment HOURS and I’ll send you 10
systems that Save Me Hours Every Week and a couple other resources to help you get started!

I used to think a messy house meant I needed better bins.⁠
Turns out, I needed a better system.⁠
Here's the difference:⁠
Storage holds your stuff.⁠
⁠
Systems tell your stuff where to go.⁠
When your home doesn't have a system, you end up asking the same questions on repeat:⁠
⁠
Where do the keys go?⁠
⁠
What do I do with today's mail?⁠
⁠
Where should I store this charger?⁠
⁠
Every "I'll just deal with it later" is a decision you didn't need to make.⁠
A good system answers the question before you ask it. The best part? Every item has a home.⁠
⁠
Then everyone knows what happens next.⁠
Less thinking. Less stress. Less clutter.⁠
⁠
This week, don't try to fix your whole house.⁠
Pick ONE area that drives you a little crazy every day, and build one simple system around it.⁠
That's it. That's the whole move.⁠
I wrote the full breakdown on the blog if you want to read more.⁠
⁠
👇 Comment SYSTEMS and I'll send you 3 of my favorite ways to get started right now (including the link to the blog post).

📌 Save this for July! Instead of starting the month wondering what needs attention, start with a plan.

The free July Homekeeping Calendar includes the Daily and Weekly Tasks — the foundation of the Clean Mama Routine.

These are the small, predictable tasks that: ✔️ keep your home functioning ✔️ prevent buildup ✔️ remove daily guesswork ✔️ help you stay consistent
 Just a simple rhythm.
Print it. Save it. Put it somewhere visible.

Follow it for one week and notice how much lighter it feels when the decisions are already made.

Comment JULY and I’ll send you the free calendar & how to follow it.
Comment GUIDE so I teach you my system! I’ll send you my free guide so you can get started and get on with your life✨

Your bedroom doesn't need a deep clean, It just needs a little reset.⁠
⁠
If your bedroom has become the place where laundry piles up, clutter collects, and everything gets dropped at the end of the day, try this 10 minute reset.⁠
⁠
✨ Make the bed⁠
✨ Clear surface clutter⁠
✨ Tidy laundry zones⁠
✨ Wipe high-touch surfaces⁠
✨ Quick floor reset⁠
✨ Set a restful tone⁠
⁠
Comment BEDROOM and I'll send you a link to the free checklist. 🛏️⁠
Save this for tonight.

Some messes need more than a quick wipe.

I put the Tineco Streech FLOOR ONE S7 Steam to the test with melted chocolate, softened butter, and one of my least-favorite cleaning jobs—behind the beverage fridge.

What impressed me most is the steam function. It helps loosen stuck-on messes while cleaning at the same time, making deep cleaning feel much more manageable.

As someone who loves simple systems and efficient cleaning, I appreciate anything that helps me get the job done faster without adding extra steps.

Tell me - what’s the toughest floor mess you’ve had to clean lately?  Comment STEAM and I’ll send you the link and a coupon code for the Tineco!

#Tineco #TinecoPartner #SteamCleaning #CleanMama #CleaningTips Homekeeping DeepCleaning FloorCleaning
